rationale: The citing paper uses Danaher et al. (2015) in its core literature-positioning section and in Table 1 to characterize prior deal-oriented coupon research, including specific redemption drivers (timing, distance, face value, product type). It then builds its contribution against that stream by arguing that prior work emphasizes coupon/deal effects and focal redemption outcomes, whereas this paper studies inspirational content and broader spending via goal completion. So the citation is not just a passing mention; it helps define the gap and the paper's claimed advance.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the deal-oriented mobile coupon literature represented by Danaher et al. (2015) by moving from coupon redemption determinants to a comparison of inspirational versus deal communication and by testing broader spending effects and goal-completion mechanisms.
